title: Israel Strikes 120 Hezbollah Targets in Southern Lebanon as Projectile Launches Resume
summary: The IDF reported striking approximately 120 Hezbollah targets in southern Lebanon within 24 hours, dismantling 70 military structures and 50 infrastructure sites, while intercepting projectile launches from Lebanon.
contents:
## IDF Intensifies Southern Lebanon Campaign

### Strike Tempo
The Israel Defense Forces reported striking approximately 120 Hezbollah targets across southern Lebanon in a single day. The operations dismantled roughly 70 military structures and approximately 50 Hezbollah infrastructure sites across several areas, according to the IDF.

### Projectile Launches
Air raid sirens sounded in the Avivim area of northern Israel as the IDF identified several projectiles launched from Lebanon. The Israeli Air Force intercepted one launch, with results of additional interceptions under review. The military characterized the incident as "an additional violation of the ceasefire understandings by the Hezbollah terrorist organization."

### Casualties Mount
Al Jazeera reported at least 41 people killed as Israel launched 50 air strikes on southern Lebanon in 24 hours, despite the ceasefire framework in place since April 16. The cumulative death toll since the March 2 escalation reached 2,659 people according to Lebanese health officials.

### Forced Displacement
The Israeli military issued new warnings threatening attacks on 12 towns and villages in southern Lebanon, ordering residents to flee. Affected locations include al-Duwayr, Arab Salim, al-Sharqiya, Jibshit, Braashit, Sarafand, and several others in the Nabatieh area.

**Sources:** TASS, Al Jazeera, IDF Spokesperson
